sections.paragraph:
δωρεά, ᾶς, ἡ (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; ins [e.g. Res Gestae of Caesar Augustus ch. 15 ‘grant’], pap, LXX, Philo, Joseph.; Ath., R. 67, 6; loanw. in rabb.) that which is given or transferred freely by one pers. to another, gift, bounty, of God (Pla., Leg. 2, 672a; Diod S 3, 47, 3; Athen. 15, 48, 693d τὴν τοῦ θεοῦ δωρεάν; Herm. Wr. 4, 5; Philo, Poster. Cai. 81 δωρεαὶ … τ. θεοῦ καλαὶ πᾶσαι; Jos., Ant. 3, 223) J 4:10; Ac 8:20. Pl. 1 Cl 19:2; 23:2; 32:1; 35:4; δ. διδόναι (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; SIG 1118, 3; Tat.; Mel., P. 88, 665; 90, 674) Ac 11:17. ἀντιλέγειν τῇ δ. τοῦ θεοῦ oppose the gift of God ISm 7:1. W. χάρις (Demosth. 21, 172; Polyb. 1, 31, 6; Diod S 3, 73, 6; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 26; Jos., Ant. 5, 54 θεοῦ χ. καὶ δ.) Ro 5:17; MPol 20:2; τῆς δ. πνευματικῆς χάριν λαμβάνειν receive the favor of the spiritual gift B 1:2; ἡ δ. ἐν χάριτι the gift given in generosity Ro 5:15; ἐπὶ τῇ ἀνεκδιηγήτῳ δ. for the indescribable gift 2 Cor 9:15; δ. ἐπουράνιος the heavenly gift Hb 6:4. W. gen. δ. τοῦ πνεύματος receive the Spirit as a gift Ac 2:38; cp. 10:45. ἡ ἔμφυτος δ. τῆς διδαχῆς B 9:9; δ. δικαιοσύνης the gift of uprightness Ro 5:17; δ. τῆς χάριτος the gift of grace Eph 3:7. κατὰ τὸ μέτρον τῆς δ. τοῦ Χριστοῦ acc. to the measure that Christ has given 4:7.—For the acc. δωρεάν used as adv., s. next entry.—DDunn, ET 81, ’69/70, 349–51; RAC X 685–703.—DELG s.v. δίδωμι B. M-M. TW.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

  1. ch. = chapter(s)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

pers. pers. = person(s)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

acc. to acc. to = according to

acc. acc. = accusative

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 266
